resources.string: Permission denied. Please check your settings.
You are not authenticated. Please try logging in again.
Something went wrong. Please try again.
Lockdown phase! Stop turning eggs and increase humidity.
Getting close! Prepare your brooder area.
Mid-incubation: Candle your eggs to check for development.
Early days: Ensure temperature and humidity are stable. Turn eggs regularly.
Focus on hybrid vigor\u2014Nature\'s way of keepin\' things strong.
By crossin\' these different lines, we\'re likely to see a boost in health and unique trait combinations. It\'s an ethical way to keep the flock robust.
Workin\' towards that perfect standard, one generation at a time.
We\'re linkin\' up similar birds to lock in these traits. High confidence in the breed markers here, though external factors always play a part.
A practical plan for temperament and size.
Balance is the key to a happy yard. We\'re lookin\' for good manners and steady growth across the generations.
Select birds and goals to refine this scenario.
Scenario match for %1$s: Based on this goal, breeds like Leghorn or Rhode Island Red would be suitable candidates.
Patience is a virtue, especially on Day %1$d. Keep that humidity steady. A spike now can cause trouble later.
Lockdown time is near. Stop turnin\' \'em soon and trust the process. The chicks are startin\' their final stretch.
Day %1$d... half-way there. Keep the air flowin\' and your eyes on the temp.
A flock of %1$d layers is a busy yard. High-calcium feed and plenty of light\'ll keep those baskets full, but don\'t forget to let \'em rest come winter.
Breeding birds need their space. Proper ratios-one sire to about 8 or 10 dams-keep the peace and ensure your fertility stays high.
Consistent growth is the goal here. Keep \'em movin\' to fresh grass if you can; it makes for a better bird and a healthier yard.
A mixed flock of %1$d is a joy to watch. Just make sure everyone has enough room to scratch without pecking their neighbors.

Don\'t forget to check the %1$s brooder today! To keep \'em adjustin\' right, aim for about %2$dC. Nature\'s waitin\' for \'em, but they need us to help \'em get used to the fresh air slow and steady.
Day %1$d in the brooder. Temp\'s at %2$dC now; try keepin\' it around %3$dC so the chicks stay comfortable.
A %1$d%% hatch rate!! You\'ve done the birds proud. It looks like our plan is likely trackin\' towards the standard.
Nature threw us a bit of a challege this time with a %1$d%% success rate. Let\'s look at the data-perhaps it was the incubation, or maybe the genetics need fresh blood.
